description: College-style coding drills: lesson, drill, trick, repair, and hidden executable checks from one source.

concepts.lesson: Append input.item to state.inventory only when it is not already present. Return the final inventory length.

choices.text: Return state.inventory.length + 1 whenever input.item exists.
choices.reason: This predicts a length but does not mutate state.inventory.

choices.text: Push input.item every time and return the length.
choices.reason: This creates duplicates when the item already exists.

choices.text: Push input.item only if state.inventory does not include it, then return state.inventory.length.
choices.reason: This preserves uniqueness and produces the required state mutation.

choices.text: Create a copied array with the new item and return the copied length.
choices.reason: This may compute the right value while failing the mutation contract.

concepts.lesson: Require every string in input.required to exist in state.flags. If all are present, add input.reward to state.rewards only when missing.

choices.text: Loop over input.flags because flags are mentioned in the prompt.
choices.reason: The prompt's input field is input.required; input.flags does not exist.

choices.text: Return true when requirements pass but do not add the reward.
choices.reason: The executable contract requires state.rewards mutation on success.

choices.text: Require every input.required flag, preserve state on failure, add input.reward once on success.
choices.reason: This matches both the boolean decision and the state mutation contract.

choices.text: Always push input.reward before checking flags.
choices.reason: Failure cases must not mutate state.rewards.

concepts.lesson: Use input.roll, not state.roll, to select the first option whose cumulative weight is greater than the roll.

choices.text: Compare cumulative weight to state.roll.
choices.reason: The roll belongs to input; state must remain unchanged and may not contain roll.

choices.text: Return the last option whenever roll is nonzero.
choices.reason: This ignores cumulative thresholds.

choices.text: Accumulate weights and return the first option id where input.roll is less than the cumulative total.
choices.reason: This implements the weighted threshold rule and preserves state.

choices.text: Sort options before choosing.
choices.reason: The prompt says first option by cumulative order; sorting changes semantics.

concepts.lesson: Return the last element of input.items, or null when the array is empty.

choices.text: Return input.items[input.items.length].
choices.reason: Array indexes are zero-based; length is one past the final index.

choices.text: Return input.items[input.items.length - 1] when non-empty, otherwise null.
choices.reason: This handles both normal and empty inputs.

choices.text: Return input.items[0].
choices.reason: That returns the first element, not the last.

choices.text: Mutate state.last and return it.
choices.reason: The task does not require state mutation.

concepts.lesson: When the prompt says do not mutate state, return a derived value without changing the object.

choices.text: Set state.total and return it.
choices.reason: This mutates state even though the prompt forbids mutation.

choices.text: Compute input.values.reduce(...) and return the sum.
choices.reason: This returns the derived value while preserving state.

choices.text: Push the sum into input.values.
choices.reason: This mutates input and changes the data shape.

choices.text: Return state.total without reading input.
choices.reason: This ignores the task input.
